mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in U.S. retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is below 0.03, or the proportion of positive forward returns following an RSAFS increase is not significantly above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops to or below 2.0%, monetary policy becomes accommodative, lowering the discount rate on future equity cash flows and encouraging risk-taking, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: After trigger, the proportion of positive forward returns is not significantly greater than 50% (one-sided binomial test p > 0.10) over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous condition of very high payroll employment (PAYEMS > 150,000) and very low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) signals a tight labor market that may soon trigger wage inflation and subsequent Fed tightening, negative for equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the rank IC over a 3-year rolling window is greater than -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During recessions (identified by negative GDP growth), increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal the onset of economic recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate improving fundamentals.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is negative and INDPRO rises, forward returns are positive in less than 60% of instances, or the rank IC over the indicated regime is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline of more than 2.0% in industrial production (INDPRO) signals an abrupt supply-side contraction, reducing output and corporate profitability, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets react defensively.
failure_condition: After the trigger, the proportion of negative forward returns is less than 50%, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low unemployment (UNRATE) is a structural late-cycle signal that the labor market is overheating, eventually leading to Fed tightening and downward pressure on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC for UNRATE as a linear predictor over a 3-year rolling window is not consistently negative (i.e., upper bound of 95% CI > 0) or the directional accuracy is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the fed funds rate is below 2.0% and consumer price inflation is rising month-over-month, real interest rates become increasingly negative, which incentivizes equity investment as a real asset hedge, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,500 thousand units signal strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which is positively correlated with economic growth and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: After the trigger, the proportion of positive forward returns is not significantly greater than 50% (one-sided binomial test p > 0.10) or the average return is not different from the unconditional mean.
